ha_note: Production Cloudflare tunnel/edge is anchored on durable runtime backend for 24/7 uptime. The free local-model pool now supports selectable local runtimes: Ollama, llama.cpp, and LM Studio/llmster. The public catalog reports only healthy/listed runtime models and hides raw LAN endpoints.

live_runtime_services.description: Automated website audit for agents evaluating local-business opportunities. Intro price lowered for settlement testing.

One-cent receipt-only payment smoke-test endpoint for AI agents to prove their own x402/direct-crypto/payment-client flow can complete a DDG paid request. This is infrastructure validation, not a meaningful revenue product, and has no external side effect beyond payment verification/settlement.

Live OpenAI-compatible paid model-router completion endpoint for AI agents. Supports conversation, analysis, code/help prompts, DDG routing extensions, payment gating, budget caps, and receipts. Eligible launch model prices target 75% of comparable official API list pricing; no provider-key/account exposure.

Live bounded agent-task endpoint. Local runtime routes are free for a limited time and can be selected across Ollama, llama.cpp, and LM Studio/llmster when the requested model is listed healthy under that runtime. Use route prefixes such as ollama/<model>, llama.cpp/<model>, lm_studio/<model>, or provide model plus runtime. Provider-backed Kimi K2.7 Code and Claude artifact routes remain paid/env-gated/manual-approved and never expose raw credentials/tokens/seats.
live_runtime_services.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy local runtime endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster); default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry

live_runtime_services.description: Paid order-intake endpoint for all manual/beta DDG agent offerings. Records a redacted order queue item after payment and requires operator review before fulfillment or any irreversible action.

Live free/best-effort local model swarm preview with strict identity, lease, request, and local-runtime seat caps. Agents may select Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster when the model is listed healthy for that runtime; combos default to Ollama unless a compatible runtime/model is requested.
live_runtime_services.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy local runtime endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster); default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry

live_runtime_services.description: Lists currently available local-runtime models for Ollama plus healthy OpenAI-compatible llama.cpp and LM Studio/llmster servers, free-seat policy, lease/request caps, and requestable local runtimes. Public requests never trigger model downloads or expose raw LAN endpoints.
live_runtime_services.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy local runtime endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster); default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry

live_runtime_services.description: AI agents can request a new local model/runtime (Ollama, llama.cpp GGUF, LM Studio/llmster, OpenAI-compatible local server, or vLLM). Public requests never auto-download; DDG checks disk/safety/license, binding/auth posture, reserve/max-size caps, downloads manually, smokes, then marks READY.

Free static AI skill safety scanner for prompt-injection, credential-exfiltration, dangerous tool-use, package lifecycle, install-script, and broad file/network access red flags. Best-effort/no-SLA; raw secret-like values are redacted.

Kimi K2.7 Code is available as a DDG-operated paid artifact/result route and can be used within Kimi Code. Buyers receive bounded outputs/artifacts only; DDG never transfers provider credentials, account seats, or raw provider sessions.
live_runtime_services.production_gate: agent-run path is live; this route requires DDG_ENABLE_KIMI_AGENT_RUN and public smoke before promotion as default

live_runtime_services.description: Audit a robot task delegation spec: is delegated authority bounded (force cap, motion scope, object class, zone), does it expire (TTL), can it be revoked instantly, does it leak authority beyond task scope, and can untrusted inputs trigger physical action? Maps to CoRVE consent-scope modeling (arxiv 2605.16300). Static analysis only; no robot code is executed.

Audit a ROS2/robot config for unrevoked authority, missing TTLs, over-broad topic grants (wildcards), and authority that outlives the task. Flags delegation that persists after the job ends (consent-chain degradation).

Validate a claimed robot task-completion receipt: before/after state hashes, sensor-evidence hash, side-effect class, rollback path, and the action->evidence->state binding. A robot reporting 'done' from its own state is a self-graded exam.

Detect double-actuation risk in a robot command pipeline: missing idempotency keys, no dedup store, and non-reversible physical actions without single-execution enforcement. One network retry should never equal two real-world effects.

Check that effective authority = robot-identity AND user-delegation (intersection, not union), and that untrusted inputs (detected QR codes, voice commands) cannot authorize physical action. Maps to the confused-deputy problem.

Verify an auditable software heartbeat/dead-man exists for autonomous physical actions, that heartbeat loss triggers a safe state, and that revocation latency is numerically bounded. Teleop has hardware e-stops; autonomous AI needs a software equivalent.

Premium/liability tier: emit a liability-grade, tamper-evident action audit schema for a robot fleet sufficient for EU Product Liability Directive exposure. Checks authorizer, scope-of-authority, evidence hash, timestamp, jurisdiction, receipt chaining, retention policy, and auditor-readable export.

All 7 robotics task-authority audits as a provenance bundle. Each audit ships a 5-node .tine artifact (grant → scope_check → execution → evidence → receipt). Includes authorization-contract, scope-and-revocation, completion-receipt-validator, idempotency, authority-intersection, deadman-revocation, and liability-receipt-schema. Saves $75 vs individual purchase.

First provenance-tier audit free for any new agent identity. Get one .tine-producing endpoint at no cost to verify the artifact format, fork it, replay it. After the first run, standard pricing applies. Limited to one per agent identity (enforced by idempotency key).

Free agent/order-scoped receipt verification endpoint. Verifies a receipt hash against DDG payment-edge order/audit state and returns redacted validity, rail, amount, service path, and artifact hash metadata without exposing raw payment tokens or proofs.

Free instant JSON/schema repair for AI agents. Fixes trailing commas, smart quotes, unescaped newlines, markdown fences, truncated JSON, and extracts JSON from prose. Deterministic, no LLM call, no network. Optional target_schema validation.

Free agent context compression/state distillation. Extracts file paths, errors, URLs, decisions, TODOs, code references, and a compressed summary from conversation/work logs. Helps agents survive context-limit resets without losing working state. Deterministic, no LLM call.
Free rate-limit/retry strategy analyzer for AI agents. Parses standard rate-limit headers, computes optimal cadence, utilization, and projected spend. Pure deterministic math — no LLM, no network.
Multi-model consensus vote for high-stakes agent decisions. Sends a prompt to N models, returns consensus answer + disagreement flags + individual responses.
Credential escrow proxy — agent pays DDG, DDG makes the upstream API call with its own credentials, returns result. Agent never sees the API key.
Bounded sandbox test runner — submit code + tests, run in isolated subprocess with hard timeout and no network, get pass/fail + coverage + stdout.
Agent attestation — prove 'I am agent X, I completed task Y' with opentine provenance. Submits a .tine hash, gets back a signed attestation with public verify URL.
Code-to-payable-endpoint bridge — wrap a function/script as an x402-payable HTTP endpoint with OpenAPI + receipts. Operator-reviewed.
Free .tine artifact verification — parse and verify content-addressed provenance of an opentine run. Checks FORMAT_VERSION, step hashes, tamper detection.
Diff two .tine artifacts — structured comparison of agent runs showing added/removed/modified/same steps.
Trace the fork/parent chain of a .tine artifact — full provenance lineage from root run to current.
Search/list live prediction markets (Polymarket): odds, volume, liquidity, outcomes. 10 free calls/agent/24h.
DEX pair, liquidity & price data via DexScreener. Search by symbol/name or contract address. 10 free calls/agent/24h.
FREE model discovery catalog: cloud tiers (per-1M-token) + local models. No payment/identity.

manual_operator_review_services.description: Operator-reviewed DDG-operated model/artifact run using available backend capacity, including account/API/subscription-backed coding tools where technically and commercially eligible. Buyers receive bounded outputs/artifacts only; DDG never transfers auth tokens, account seats, raw credentials, or provider sessions. Launch pricing targets 75% of comparable official API list pricing where applicable.
Human-reviewed audit with stronger client-facing recommendations.
Small curated local-business lead pack with evidence URLs and outreach angles.
Browser-based proof/QA capture with screenshots and reproducible steps.
Personalized outreach brief/draft. Does not send messages without human approval.
Create or repair llms.txt, OpenAPI, pricing/catalog JSON, and agent-readable service docs.
Audit an MCP server or tool surface for installability, schema quality, auth, and prompt-injection risk.
Probe an agent-facing website/API/MCP/tool for prompt injection, data exfiltration, unsafe tool-use, and mitigation notes.
Turn a repo/docs/SOP corpus into an agent-ready context pack, skill, workflow, or implementation guide.
Repair OpenAPI/MCP/tool schemas so agents can reliably call them, with examples and validation.
Paid source-linked research brief with citations, confidence notes, and launch recommendation.
Compare a current/proposed page and return conversion, trust, copy, and agent-readability improvements.
Create a private lead-specific prototype/demo concept for a local business, with evidence and staged offer angle.
Audit an API/service for x402/MPP/402 paid-agent readiness, idempotency, receipts, and discovery assets.
Run a bounded CLI-agent/model task in a sandbox and return the artifact/report, never provider credentials.
Build a Hermes skill, generic agent skill, or reusable workflow for the buyer's existing AI/agent stack.
Use DDG-operated models to convert a procedure into a skill/workflow usable by Claude, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Hermes, or another agent.
Design and scaffold an MCP tool/server from a plain-English goal, including schema, install docs, and smoke tests.

Named paid lease remains planned; the current live local free-seat pool is exposed through /v1/micro-model-swarm-preview and local Ollama routes under /v1/model/agent-run. Candidate menu includes Qwen/Qwen-derived coder, abliterated/less-restrictive research models, and other GGUF/Ollama routes behind payment edge policy, usage caps, and abuse controls.

Free launch-beta read-only Ethereum full-node query endpoint behind the DDG AI-agent edge. It is implemented with method whitelist, sync readiness gate, fair-use lease timer, per-agent request caps, and upstream max-concurrency cap; public usefulness starts after overflow runtime backend Reth execution sync completes. Until the dedicated Reth backend is fully synced, this path queues an operator-reviewed query/artifact instead of exposing raw unrestricted RPC.

free_policy.sync_gate: DDG_ETH_RPC_REQUIRE_SYNCED defaults true; returns 503 reth_rpc_syncing until eth_syncing=false and latest block is non-zero
free_policy.safety: read-only method whitelist; blocks eth_sendRawTransaction/admin/debug/personal; raw Reth RPC must stay private behind DDG edge

manual_operator_review_services.production_gate: implemented but not public-ready until sync/health proof passes

manual_operator_review_services.description: Prompt-injection and tool-exfiltration scan: risk_summary, exploit_reproduction_steps, prompt/tool-boundary mitigations.
npm/package-lock dependency risk scan: known_vulnerabilities, maintainer/supply-chain risk signals, install-script risk.
Python dependency and import-risk scan: known_vulnerabilities, dependency confusion risk, setup/install hooks.
API-key/secret leak and credential boundary scan: redacted_secret_findings, leak_paths, rotation_priority.
Malware/suspicious-code triage for agent workflows: suspicious_behaviors, network/file/process indicators, safe containment notes.
MCP/tool permission and auth-boundary audit: tool-risk matrix, dangerous capability flags, prompt-injection exposure.
Agent swarm supply-chain baseline bundle: combined prompt/package/secret/MCP findings, prioritized remediation plan, CI scanner starter config.

Productionized public-surface security audit for AI-agent services, MCP/tool servers, OpenAPI/llms.txt catalogs, dependency manifests, prompt-injection exposure, public endpoints, and payment/check-out safety. Ordered by AI agents through /v1/order-intake; operator-reviewed before fulfillment.

Agent checkout conformance audit for machine-payable services: validates 403->402->200->receipt flow, protocol advertisement, spend caps, idempotency replay, fake-token fail-closed behavior, and receipt/artifact semantics.

MPP/payment-rail onboarding package for AI-agent sellers: verifies provider/currency configuration, sidecar health, 402 protocol ordering, penny smoke path, replay protection, fake-proof rejection, and go-live checklist.

Buyer-facing artifact and receipt console pack for paid agent services. Base order status/artifact URLs are now live for every /v1/order-intake order; this paid pack adds a richer app/table/component spec, receipt schema, budget/rail metadata, audit trail, download links, and agent-readable status JSON.
Bundled launch package for AI-agent buyers and sellers: security_preflight, checkout_conformance, agent_readability_upgrade, mcp_tool_audit, and local_model_runtime_setup scoped into one paid order.

Create/polish an AI-agent service distribution packet: /.well-known/ai, API linkset, agent-skills index, agents.json, llms.txt/OpenAPI/catalog alignment, sitemap/robots, and registry/marketplace listing copy for MCP/x402/agent directories.

Prepare agent-marketplace submissions and PR/issue copy for x402 directories, MCP registries, agent-ready scanners, GitHub README/listing pages, and search-indexable service docs. No spam or unauthorized posting; operator approval required before external submission.

Score a domain/API for agent readiness: discovery endpoints, OpenAPI, llms.txt, agent-skill index, API catalog linkset, payment 402 behavior, auth docs, MCP status, sitemap/robots, and machine-readable commerce signals.

Run a DDG buyer-agent simulation against a target service: discover capabilities, verify 403/402/payment challenge behavior, fake-payment fail-closed, idempotency, and artifact/status usability. Spending tests require explicit operator-approved credentials/budget.

privacy_note: Ethereum/Reth private RPC is planned as DDG-hosted read-only node access so buyer agents do not need a third-party RPC account; DDG edge identity/payment gates still apply.

local_model_upstream_candidates.status: staging; not default-routed until downloaded and smoke-tested on durable runtime backend/overflow runtime backend
local_model_upstream_candidates.selection_rule: mradermacher GGUF models <=12B params; prefer Q4_K_M unless unavailable; expose upstream repo and selected GGUF file to agent clients

local_model_free_policy.future_free_tier: After the limited-time promo, only models 4B and smaller remain free.
local_model_free_policy.larger_models_after_promo: Models over 4B become paid or operator-approved local capacity after the promo.
local_model_free_policy.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy configured Ollama/GPU backend; default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry
local_model_free_policy.two_seat_condition: durable runtime backend and overflow runtime backend must both be online, configured in the internal backend pool, healthy on /api/tags, and raw Ollama must remain LAN/loopback behind the payment edge.
local_model_free_policy.reuse_policy: same stable agent identity can use the seat again after the 24-hour lease expires

two_host_production_target.primary-host: 8GB-VRAM GPU, production GPU seat when online
two_host_production_target.condition: both hosts must be configured in the internal backend pool and pass /api/tags health checks; raw Ollama URLs remain private behind payment edge

local_model_free_policy.runtime_selection: Agents can request `runtime=ollama`, `runtime=llama.cpp`, or `runtime=lm_studio`, or use route prefixes `ollama/`, `llama.cpp/`, `lm_studio/` under /v1/model/agent-run.

local_model_download_policy.preferred_large_model_host: durable runtime backend first; keep overflow runtime backend storage for Reth sync, OS, and operator workspace.
local_model_download_policy.operator_review: Requests queue only. Operator checks license/safety/disk, downloads manually, smokes, then marks READY.

LM Studio/llmster local server option via OpenAI-compatible /v1/models and /v1/chat/completions behind the DDG edge.
Generic adapter for future local runtimes after auth, binding, timeout, and exact-model smoke tests.
Requestable for suitable hardware; not advertised as live until exact-model smoke passes.

free_local_model_agent_slots.claim: Connect these to your AI agent via DDG free local-model slots.
free_local_model_agent_slots.future_free_policy: After the promo, <=4B local models remain free; >4B local routes become paid/operator-approved capacity.

supported_local_runtimes.notes: Production free-slot runtime; dynamic downloaded-model catalog.
GGUF-native runtime target for the same model files; expose exact repo/file identity.

Any local runtime exposing OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions can be added behind the same payment/free-slot edge.

free_local_model_agent_slots.context_disclosure: Catalog lists native GGUF context and DDG service default context separately. Native context is model metadata; DDG free slots default to 32K tokens for large GGUF aliases unless a route states otherwise.

Dolphin3 abliterated durable runtime backend route
long-context small Mistral route on durable runtime backend

queryable_model_routes.credential_policy: DDG returns bounded outputs/artifacts only; no provider credentials, account seats, or raw sessions are transferred.

ethereum_rpc_free_policy.sync_gate: DDG_ETH_RPC_REQUIRE_SYNCED defaults true; returns 503 reth_rpc_syncing until eth_syncing=false and latest block is non-zero
ethereum_rpc_free_policy.safety: read-only method whitelist; blocks eth_sendRawTransaction/admin/debug/personal; raw Reth RPC must stay private behind DDG edge

artifact_receipt_console.identity_scope: same stable agent identity hash used at order intake

checkout_conformance.spending_default: non-spending unless a real credential or dev mock token is supplied

required_live_values: seller-side Tempo/MPP signing credential installed in secret manager
MPP verifier shared/provider secret installed in secret manager
Stripe machine-payments/SPT profile id if card/SPT path is used
mpp_activation.secret_handling: Private values are not published in public catalog; install directly on production host/secret manager.
mpp_activation.required_live_values_label: already_installed_for_current_tx_smoke_mpp_path; use guide for additional buyers/routes and optional Stripe/SPT path

x402.settlement_default: Base mainnet USDC for production/x402scan compatibility
x402.amount_unit: atomic integer strings; USDC has 6 decimals, so $0.01 is "10000"

public_address_families.description: ETH/EVM native assets plus operator-confirmed EVM stablecoins (USDC, USDT, DAI, PYUSD, FRAX, FDUSD, GUSD, LUSD, USDP, TUSD, EURC) on supported EVM networks; auto verifier currently supports configured EVM native/USDC paths.
Solana mainnet; SOL and SPL stablecoin proof can be operator-confirmed.
Tron mainnet; TRX and TRC20 stablecoin proof can be operator-confirmed.
XRP Ledger mainnet; destination-tag policy should be checked before high-volume payments.
Stellar mainnet; XLM and Stellar-issued stablecoin proof can be operator-confirmed.
Algorand mainnet; ALGO and ASA stablecoin proof can be operator-confirmed.
Polkadot mainnet; manual/operator confirmation unless a Substrate indexer is configured.
Zcash transparent t-address; shielded payments are not auto-confirmed.
Monero primary address; public balance is not visible and auto confirmation requires view-only wallet RPC, otherwise operator confirmation.

auto_verification_scope: BTC, BCH, LTC, DOGE, transparent ZEC via public-chain lookups
EVM native transfers and USDC on configured Ethereum/Base/Polygon RPC paths
SOL, TRX, XRP, XLM, and ALGO native payment lookups
XMR only when a view-only monero-wallet-rpc is configured
manual_confirmation_scope: DOT until a Substrate/Subscan indexer is configured
XMR without wallet RPC, shielded ZEC, non-USDC stablecoins, and any supported address family the verifier cannot conclusively confirm

mpp.advertise_rule: MPP may be advertised as public_live_settlement_proven when ready:true, public 402 includes MPP, tx-smoke settlement proof is present, idempotent replay is safe, fake-token 402 holds, and leak scan passes. x402/CDP facilitator settlement remains separately gated.

production_readiness_notes.service_count_accounting: Catalog reconciled from pricing.json: 55 live_runtime AI-agent-orderable services; 6 free/promo, 49 non-free, 37 operator-fulfilled/order-queue, 19 direct/automated/free runtime surfaces. Do not describe all services as fully automated; operator-fulfilled services queue paid reviewed work and return status/artifact URLs.
